items.title: Sigmoid
items.description: Sigmoid saturates at both tails with gradients near zero; ReLU stays linear and unsaturated for positive inputs, which is why ReLU replaced sigmoid in hidden layers of deep networks.

items.title: GELU
items.description: GELU is smooth and non-monotonic, common in transformer FFN blocks; ReLU is piecewise linear and the default in CNNs and older MLPs—choice depends on architecture and empirical performance.

items.title: Applying activations
items.description: Forward propagation computes z = Wx + b then applies f(z); this topic defines ReLU and variants as choices for f at each hidden layer.

items.title: Gradient descent
items.description: ReLU's derivative (0 or 1) scales gradients during backprop; dying ReLU neurons pass zero gradient to their weights, stalling gradient descent updates for those units.

items.title: Vanishing and exploding gradients — causes
items.description: ReLU mitigates sigmoid-style vanishing gradients on the positive branch, but deep ReLU nets can still suffer exploding gradients or effective vanishing via dead neurons.
